source: Kimi extended research — Chinese and Asian reception of Torah and NT figures
note: Supplemental layer only. No core findings depend on this data.

chinese_islamic_hui.finding: Torah figures appear only through Quranic mediation in Han Kitab texts
chinese_islamic_hui.gap: No Torah-specific engagement documented. No NT figures in Han Kitab texts. All biblical figures presented exclusively through Quranic reception.
chinese_islamic_hui.academic_source: Frankel, James. Rectifying God's Name. University of Hawai'i Press, 2011.

nestorian_christian_tang.finding: Documented NT figures in Nestorian Stele (781 CE) and Dunhuang manuscripts
primary_sources: Nestorian Stele (大秦景教流行中国碑), 781 CE
Zunjing (尊經) Diptychs, early 10th century CE
nestorian_christian_tang.numerical_note: Stele mentions '27 scriptures' (二十七部) — possibly corresponding to 27 NT books though disputed (Church of the East canonized only 22). Number 365 appears as 'forms of error'. No systematic isopsephy or figure-level numerical treatment documented.
nestorian_christian_tang.gap: No complete biblical texts survive from Tang Nestorian period. Only titles and fragments.
nestorian_christian_tang.academic_source: Saeki, P.Y. The Nestorian Documents and Relics in China. 2nd ed. Tokyo Academy of Oriental Culture, 1951.

kaifeng_jewish.finding: Four stelae (1489-1679 CE) document Torah figure transliterations and genealogical numerology
primary_sources: Memorial Book of the Dead, mid-17th century CE

notable_findings.finding: Adam identified with Pan Gu (盘古)
notable_findings.note: Documented syncretic identification of Adam with Chinese creation myth progenitor
notable_findings.sirr_note: Two traditions placing the first human at the origin of all humanity

notable_findings.note: Kaifeng community independently arrived at 19 as generational distance from Adam to Abraham
notable_findings.sirr_note: 19 appears independently in: أيوب Abjad raw value (19), Q 74:30 (nineteen), Ἰούδας isopsephy chain (685→19→10→1). Record only. No causal claim.

notable_findings.finding: Moses dated to 613th year of Zhou dynasty
notable_findings.note: Community deliberately encoded 613 mitzvot into the chronological placement of the lawgiver
notable_findings.sirr_note: Intentional internal numerology by the community itself — not an external structural finding

kaifeng_jewish.gap: No theological writings survive. No Chinese translation of Torah. No systematic symbolic treatment of individual figures beyond genealogical placement.
academic_sources: Leslie, Donald D. The Survival of the Chinese Jews: The Jewish Community of Kaifeng. Leiden: Brill, 1972.
Leslie, Donald D. The Chinese-Hebrew Memorial Book of the Jewish Community of Kaifeng. Belconnen, 1984.

summary.chinese_islamic: No Torah/NT engagement — all biblical figures through Quranic mediation only
summary.nestorian: NT figures documented in Tang dynasty stele and Dunhuang fragments — no systematic numerical treatment
summary.kaifeng: Torah figures documented with transliterations and genealogical numerology — Adam=Pan Gu, Abraham=19th generation, Moses=613th year
summary.overall_confidence: LOW — supplemental metadata only. No core scripture module findings depend on this layer.
